plants.name: Achacha
plants.origin: Amazon Basin, Bolivia (South America)
plants.economicUse: Fresh fruits are economical/commercial, orange-coloured, egg-shaped with sweet-tart flavour. Used in making fresh juice, jams, jellies & desserts. Recently commercialized in Australia.
plants.medicalSignificance: Traditionally known in Bolivian folk medicine for digestive issues, anti-inflammatory effects, and appetite suppression. It contains hydroxycitric acid (HCA), similar to Garcinia cambogia, though at lower concentrations. Marketed in some supplements for weight management.

plants.name: Grumichama
plants.vernacularName: Grumichama Cherry / Brazilian Cherry / Spanish Cherry / Grumixama
plants.origin: Atlantic Rainforests (Brazil)
plants.economicUse: Fresh fruits are eaten fresh and used in making jams, jellies, wine, and beverages. Attractive flowers and plants are used in subtropical landscaping.
plants.medicalSignificance: Potential application in nutraceuticals and functional medicine/foods. Rich in vitamin C, anthocyanins (purple pigments), polyphenols, and antioxidants. Has antimicrobial properties. Used in treating diarrhea and gastrointestinal discomfort.

plants.name: Savannah Cherry
plants.origin: Tropical Savanna, Brazilian Cerrado Biome
plants.economicUse: Small red fruits are sweet–sour, juicy, high in vitamin C & antioxidants. Eaten fresh and used in jams, jellies, and juices. It is a flavoring, coloring agent, and natural antioxidant for beverages and desserts.
plants.medicalSignificance: Used in Brazilian folk medicine. Potential superfruit due to high phenolic, flavonoid, and vitamin content. Rich in antioxidants, antibacterial, antifungal, anti-inflammatory, hypoglycemic and anti-diabetic.

plants.name: Jaboticaba
plants.origin: Brazil particularly Sao Paulo, Argentina
plants.economicUse: Fruits are used in jams, jellies, liquers, preserves and beverages.
plants.medicalSignificance: Rich in Vit C, anthocyanins, anti-oxidants, antimicrobials, anti-inflammatory. Treats diarrhea, asthma, tonsillitis, blood sugar control.

plants.name: Surinam Cherry
plants.origin: Brazil particularly Uruguay, Paraguay, Northern Argentina
plants.economicUse: Fresh fruits are bright red or dark purple ribbed berries used in flavouring sweet/tangy or slightly resinous foods. Used in making jams, jellies, liqueurs, beverages. Used in making ornamental bonsai. Fresh leaves are used in making herbal teas and mosquito repellent.
plants.medicalSignificance: Extracts are anti-malarial, anti-bacterial, anti-fungal, anti-inflammatory, blood sugar regulation, anti-obesity. Has cardiovascular effects.

plants.name: Lilly Pilly Pink
plants.origin: Australia, Eastern coastal rainforests of Queensland
plants.economicUse: Fruits are sweet to tart. Used in jams, jellies, sauces, syrups, cordial, desserts. In urban landscaping used as hedge plant or agro-forestry.
plants.medicalSignificance: Traditionally used by Aboriginal Australians as medicines for coughs, diarrhea and general digestive health. Is anti-oxidative and anti-inflammatory. Rich in flavonoids, anthocyanin, Vit-C. Treats blood sugar, skin health, immune regulation.

plants.name: Beauty Berry
plants.origin: Southeastern USA especially Texas, Arkansas, Florida, Georgia
plants.economicUse: Small, metallic purple berries are mildly sweet and have astringent properties. Used in making jams, jellies, wines, herbal teas. Used in landscaping and ornamental gardens.
plants.medicalSignificance: Roots used in treating dysentery, urinary tract issue, malarial repellent, rheumatism. Compounds like callicarpenal and intermedeol have insect-repelling activity. Leaves are anti-oxidative and anti-inflammatory.

plants.name: Mangosteen
plants.origin: Malay Peninsula, including Indonesia, Malaysia, Thailand, and the Philippines
plants.economicUse: Fruits are called 'Queen of fruits', fruit has white, juicy aromatic pulp. Fruits are used in making juices, jams, jellies, canned products, desserts and functional beverages. Fruit pulp is used in making nutraceuticals.
plants.medicalSignificance: Mangosteen (Vrishatphal) is mentioned in some regional and Siddha texts for treating diarrhea, skin eruptions, and inflammation. Mangosteen is classified as Kasjaya (astringent) and Tikta (bitter) in taste.

plants.name: Yellow Che Fruit
plants.origin: China, Japan & Korea
plants.economicUse: Mulberry-like fruits are mildly sweet in taste used for making jams, jellies, beverages, wines, juices.
plants.medicalSignificance: The fruits are antioxidant, liver tonic, anti-inflammatory, anticancerous, antidiabetic. Liver protective properties.

plants.name: Mamey Sapote
plants.origin: Central America & Mexico - Guatemala, Honduras, Northern South America
plants.economicUse: Fruits are known for excellent flavor, texture. Eaten fresh as smoothies, ice-creams, desserts, milkshakes and baby food. Suitable for home gardens, orchards, and agroforestry systems.
plants.medicalSignificance: Central American and Caribbean folk medicine for stomach pain, digestive disorder or liver tonic. Seeds used topically as a skin treatment.

plants.name: Australian Finger Lime
plants.origin: Eastern Australia (rainforests of Queensland and New South Wales)
plants.economicUse: Cultivated for its unique caviar-like pulp, eaten fresh and used for jams, jellies, beverages, sauces, and desserts.
plants.medicalSignificance: Finger lime has been traditionally used by Aboriginal Australians. It is used as a bush medicine for treating skin irritations and infection. High in antioxidants and Vit-C. Has antimicrobial properties, can be used in skin care products and nutraceuticals.

plants.name: Climber Mango
plants.origin: Indo-Myanmar region, Southeast Asia & Northeastern India
plants.economicUse: Economically used as a rootstock for grafting cultivated mango varieties. A great agroforestry specie with food crops.
plants.medicalSignificance: It is believed to be a folk medicine, helps with diarrhea, dysentery, and wound healing. It contains polyphenol and antioxidants.

plants.name: Lemba
plants.origin: Southeast Asia and the Indian subcontinent. Commonly in India, Sri Lanka, Indonesia
plants.economicUse: Economically used as ornamental plant due to its attractive flowers. Rhizomes are used in traditional medicines, cosmetics, shampoo and starch production.
plants.vedicSignificance: Mentioned in Ayurveda, Siddha, and Unani systems
plants.medicalSignificance: Rhizomes are used for antidiabetic (reduces blood sugar), antipyretic (fever reduction). It has anti-inflammatory, anti-oxidative, and anti-microbial properties. Used for treating skin diseases, bronchitis, intestinal worms, and urinary disorders.

plants.name: Date Palm Hybrid
plants.origin: Middle East and North Africa
plants.economicUse: It is grown for edible fruits rich in natural sugars, fiber, and minerals. Used for fresh consumption, dry fruits, and sweets. Used as agroforestry and horticulture development. It is popular in export and local markets—especially valuable in arid and semi-arid zones.
plants.medicalSignificance: Dates are widely used in Ayurveda as body tonics and aphrodisiacs. It is a digestive aid, mild laxative, and blood purifier. It is rich in iron, potassium, and antioxidants. It is helpful in anemia, fatigue, reproductive health, and heart health.

plants.name: Cedar Bay Cherry
plants.origin: Northern Australia (especially around Cedar Bay, Queensland), New Guinea, and nearby islands
plants.economicUse: It is grown for its sweet, cherry-like fruits. It is used in fresh consumption, jams, jellies and dessert.
plants.medicalSignificance: Traditionally used by Aboriginal Australians. Fruit consumed for general nourishment, leaves and bark sometimes used in folk remedies. Fruit is rich in antioxidants, Vit-C, and fiber. Potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.

plants.name: Pineapple Medura
plants.origin: South America (particularly Brazil and Paraguay)
plants.economicUse: It is grown for sweet edible, juicy fruit. Used for juices, jams, canned products, desserts, confectionery and food processing industries. It is an important horticultural crop in tropical and subtropical regions.
plants.medicalSignificance: Mentioned in Ayurveda for aiding digestion (rich in bromelain, a digestive enzyme), treating inflammation, indigestion and intestinal worms. Other properties are anti-oxidants, anti-inflammatory and immune boosting. Juice is often used to treat sore throat and digestive sluggishness.

plants.name: Barbados Acerola Cherry
plants.vernacularName: Barbados Acerola Cherry / West Indian Cherry
plants.origin: Tropical Americas – especially the Caribbean, Central America, and northern South America
plants.economicUse: Barbados cherry is grown primarily for its extremely high Vit-C content. Used in making jams, jellies, supplements, powders and health drinks. It is used in cosmetic products. Medically, used in nutraceuticals and fortified foods.
plants.medicalSignificance: Rich in antioxidants, polyphenols, carotenoids. It is used for boosting immunity, skin health/skin rejuvenation, anti-aging and wound healing. It is helpful in combating scurvy, fatigue and common colds.

plants.name: Burmese Grape
plants.origin: Indo-Myanmar region, Southeast Asia & Northeastern India; especially Myanmar, NE India, Thailand, Bangladesh, Malaysia
plants.economicUse: Burmese grape is grown for its edible fruits, sweet to slightly sour in taste. It is consumed fresh, or used in juices, jams, and pickles. Also cultivated as a shade and ornamental tree for cultivating gardens/orchards.
plants.medicalSignificance: Traditionally used in folk and tribal medicine (especially in India and Southeast Asia). Fruits aid in digestion, act as a coolant. Bark and leaves are used for treating skin ailments, diarrhea, and infections. Rich in Vitamin C, iron, and antioxidants.

plants.name: Chamaliang
plants.origin: Native to Southeast Asia, particularly Malaysia, Thailand, Indonesia, and northeastern India
plants.economicUse: Chamaliang is grown for its small, mango-like fruits. Fruits are eaten fresh, used in pickles, preserves, and desserts. Occasionally cultivated as an ornamental and fruit-bearing tree.
plants.medicalSignificance: Traditional uses in Southeast Asian folk medicine. Fruits are used for digestive aid. Leaves and bark are used to treat fever, skin infections, and cough. Rich in Vitamin C, carotenoids, and polyphenols.

plants.name: Tropical Rainforest Plum
plants.vernacularName: Tropical Rainforest Plum var. Merry Poza
plants.origin: Tropical regions of Southeast Asia, India, and Africa
plants.economicUse: Rainforest Plum is grown for edible fruits (small, tangy, plum-like). Used in jams, pickles, chutneys, and local beverages. Also planted as ornamental shrub/tree. It is used to make live fences or hedges (due to thorns).
plants.medicalSignificance: Traditionally used in folk and traditional medicine. Fruit treats digestive issues, diarrhea, and respiratory ailments. Leaves/Bark are antibacterial, antioxidant, and anti-inflammatory. Used for treating skin diseases, fever and wound healing.

plants.name: Water Apple
plants.origin: Southeast Asia – likely Malaysia, Indonesia, and the Andaman-Nicobar region
plants.economicUse: Fresh consumption: consumed raw for its crisp, watery texture. Used in making pickles, jams, jellies, preserves, juice and beverages. Also grown as an ornamental plant due to glossy leaves and attractive fruit. Used in agroforestry and landscaping.
plants.medicalSignificance: Believed to be cooling in nature and used for balancing Pitta and Kapha doshas in traditional Indian medicine. It is diuretic, aids hydration due to high water content, mildly laxative, anti-diabetic, anti-inflammatory, and leaves/bark are used for skin irritations.

plants.name: Mexican Sapote Black
plants.vernacularName: Mexican Sapote Black / Chocolate Pudding Fruit
plants.origin: Mexico, Central America, native to Southern Mexico, Guatemala & Colombia
plants.economicUse: Fruit is edible, ripe pulp resembles chocolate pudding in color and texture. Fruit is eaten fresh or used in smoothies, desserts, and baking. It is a natural sweetener, used in low-calorie dessert recipes.
plants.medicalSignificance: Fruit is rich in Vitamin-C and Vitamin-A. It strengthens immunity, supports skin health, and aids digestion due to its high fiber content. Low in fat, often recommended in weight loss diets. It is anti-inflammatory and mildly laxative. Antioxidants, carotenoids and flavonoids help regulate blood sugar and support diabetic diets in moderation.

plants.name: Jackfruit
plants.origin: Indo-Malaysia region, particularly Western Ghats, Southeast Asia including Vietnam
plants.economicUse: Ripe fruits are eaten fresh or used in desserts, ice cream and beverages. Unripe fruits are cooked as vegetables or meat substitutes. Seeds are roasted or boiled. Used in processed foods such as chips, canned jackfruit, jams and syrups. Wood is used for furniture and musical instruments. Latex is traditionally used as an adhesive.
plants.vedicSignificance: Jackfruit (Panasa) is well documented in Ayurveda. Classical texts describe it as nourishing, strengthening and cooling. Fruits, seeds, leaves and bark are used for balancing Vata and Pitta. Mentioned in Charaka Samhita and Sushruta Samhita.
plants.medicalSignificance: Rich in fiber, antioxidants and Vitamin-C. Supports digestion, boosts immunity and helps reduce inflammation. Rich in potassium for blood pressure regulation. Seeds have aphrodisiac properties in Ayurveda. Latex and bark are traditionally used for wound healing and skin diseases. Possesses anti-ulcer and antibacterial properties.

plants.name: White Sapota
plants.vernacularName: White Sapota / Casimiroa / Mexican Apple / Zapote Blanco
plants.origin: Central America and Mexico
plants.economicUse: Fruits are creamy with sweet custard-like pulp. Consumed fresh or used in smoothies, ice creams and desserts. Grown in orchards and backyard gardens. Popular in tropical and subtropical regions and increasingly cultivated for gourmet fruit markets.
plants.medicalSignificance: White Sapota is traditionally used as a mild sedative to calm nerves and aid sleep. Rich in Vitamin-C and Vitamin-A, supports immunity and eye health. Helps digestion and gut health. Traditionally used for blood sugar regulation. Anti-inflammatory and antioxidant. Rich potassium content supports healthy blood pressure.

plants.name: Avocado
plants.origin: Central America and Southern Mexico
plants.economicUse: Fresh fruit is rich and creamy. Used in salads, toast, smoothies and guacamole. Avocado oil is widely used in culinary, cosmetic and pharmaceutical products. Used in nutraceuticals. Seeds and peels are researched for antioxidant properties. It is an important export crop in tropical and subtropical countries.
plants.medicalSignificance: Avocado supports heart health through healthy monounsaturated fats and potassium. Helps lower bad cholesterol and regulate blood pressure. Rich in Vitamin-E, polyphenols, fiber, folate and Vitamin-K. Supports skin, hair, digestion, brain and bone health. Possesses antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, antibacterial and potential anti-diabetic properties.

plants.name: Lubika Sunny Sweet
plants.vernacularName: Lubika Sunny Sweet / Indian Coffee Plum
plants.origin: Tropical Asia, most likely India/Southeast Asia
plants.economicUse: Fresh fruits are eaten raw, made into pickles, chutneys, jams, marmalade, and curries. It is grown as hedge plants and sold as saplings for home orchards. Its wood is occasionally harvested for lumber.
plants.medicalSignificance: Rich in antioxidants and antimicrobial compounds, useful in managing oxidative stress and infections. Anti-diabetic, helps lower blood glucose levels. Supports digestion, relieves constipation, promotes cardiovascular health, and has traditional uses for arthritis and memory enhancement.

plants.name: Madroño
plants.origin: Tropical Americas — particularly Trinidad, Central and South Tropical America including Colombia
plants.economicUse: Fruits are consumed fresh, known for their sweet to slightly citrusy pulp with a foamy texture. Occasionally cultivated and also used as animal feed and fuelwood.
plants.medicalSignificance: Possesses antioxidative and anti-inflammatory effects. Extracts show antimicrobial and antiplatelet activity. It is hypoglycemic, antihyperlipidemic, hepatoprotective and cardioprotective. Xanthones present in the fruit exhibit anticancer, neuroprotective and metabolic benefits.

plants.name: Luc's Mexican Garcinia
plants.vernacularName: Luc's Mexican Garcinia / Mexican Mangosteen / Limoncillo
plants.origin: Mexico
plants.economicUse: Fruits are sweet with a lemony-tart flavour. Fruit is used as a weight-loss medicine. It is cultivated in fruit gardens and grown as a slow shade-loving tree.
plants.medicalSignificance: Fruit is used in weight management due to its ability to reduce appetite and inhibit fat synthesis through hydroxycitric acid. Used traditionally as an astringent for dysentery and digestive complaints. May help regulate blood sugar and cholesterol. Bark and resin are used as cathartic and purgative medicines.

plants.name: Kei Apple
plants.origin: Southern Africa, particularly Mozambique and South Africa
plants.economicUse: Fruits are edible, tart to yellow-orange, used in jams, jellies, preserves and beverages. Plants are commonly grown as protective hedges because of their dense thorny branches and are also used for ornamental landscaping.
plants.medicalSignificance: Rich in Vitamin-C, supports immunity and acts as an antioxidant. Exhibits antibacterial properties and may help in managing infections. Traditionally used for digestive health, mild laxative effects and treatment of skin ailments.

plants.name: Red/Pink Water Apple
plants.origin: Southeast Asia, particularly Malaysia, Indonesia, and the Andaman & Nicobar Islands
plants.economicUse: Fruit is eaten fresh and used in fruit salads, pickles, juices and traditional sweets. Crisp and mildly sweet. It is also grown as an ornamental tree due to its glossy leaves and attractive fruits. Commonly used in agroforestry and tropical landscaping.
plants.medicalSignificance: Fruits are rich in water and fiber, aiding hydration and digestion. Low in calories, making them suitable for weight management. Rich in antioxidants and possess mild diuretic properties supporting kidney and urinary health. Traditionally used for fever, liver health and skin care in several cultures.
